title: I am qualified to be here
subtitle: An affirmation for the moments when imposter syndrome runs loudly
excerpt: For before the pitch, the panel, the interview, the published piece, when the imposter feeling is arguing that you don't belong in the room. A plain statement against the loop.

practice.whenToUse: Before any high-stakes moment where imposter syndrome is actively arguing that you shouldn't be there

content.text: Imposter syndrome doesn't respond to argument. The loop is self-sealing and explaining your qualifications to yourself rarely reduces the feeling. This affirmation interrupts the loop with a plain statement and a small grounding action.
Say this three times, standing if you can, feet flat on the floor:
I am qualified to be here.

Then name, out loud or in writing, one specific thing you know about the subject at hand that someone newer than you doesn't yet know. Not to prove anything. Just to feel the ground of actual competence before you walk in.
